    1. isRuleApplicationSupported(TermServices, PosInOccurrence, JTerm, Rule, Goal, Object, JTerm, JTerm)

Interface ChildTermLabelPolicy

All Superinterfaces:
RuleSpecificTask
public interface ChildTermLabelPolicy extends RuleSpecificTask

A ChildTermLabelPolicy is used by TermLabelManager.instantiateLabels(TermLabelState, Services, PosInOccurrence, Rule, RuleApp, Goal, Object, JTerm, JTerm) to decide for each TermLabel on a child or grandchild of the application JTerm if it should be re-added to the new JTerm or not.

For more information about TermLabels and how they are maintained during prove read the documentation of interface TermLabel.

See Also:
  • TermLabel
  • TermLabelManager

    Description

    Decides to add or not to add the given TermLabel on a child or grandchild of the application JTerm to the new JTerm which will be created.

    Decides if the currently active Rule application is supported or not.

    Methods inherited from interface RuleSpecificTask

    getSupportedRuleNames

  • Method Details

    • isRuleApplicationSupported

      boolean isRuleApplicationSupported(TermServices services, PosInOccurrence applicationPosInOccurrence, JTerm applicationTerm, Rule rule, Goal goal, Object hint, JTerm tacletTerm, JTerm newTerm)
      Decides if the currently active Rule application is supported or not. If it is not supported no iteration over children will be executed. Only if it returns true addLabel(TermServices, PosInOccurrence, JTerm, Rule, Goal, Object, JTerm, JTerm, JTerm, TermLabel) will be called if a child JTerm contains a managed label.
      Parameters:
      services - The Services used by the Proof on which a Rule is applied right now.
      applicationPosInOccurrence - The PosInOccurrence in the previous Sequent which defines the JTerm that is rewritten.
      applicationTerm - The JTerm defined by the PosInOccurrence in the previous Sequent.
      rule - The Rule which is applied.
      goal - The optional Goal on which the JTerm to create will be used.
      hint - An optional hint passed from the active rule to describe the term which should be created.
      tacletTerm - The optional JTerm in the taclet which is responsible to instantiate the new JTerm for the new proof node or null in case of built in rules.
      newTerm - the template for the new JTerm to create
      Returns:
      true keep TermLabel and add it to the new JTerm. false drop TermLabel and do not need it to the new JTerm.

    • addLabel

      boolean addLabel(TermServices services, PosInOccurrence applicationPosInOccurrence, JTerm applicationTerm, Rule rule, Goal goal, Object hint, JTerm tacletTerm, JTerm newTerm, JTerm childTerm, TermLabel label)

      Decides to add or not to add the given TermLabel on a child or grandchild of the application JTerm to the new JTerm which will be created.

      If the child JTerm is still a child of the new JTerm the label will still exist independent from the result of this method on the child. To remove it from the child a refacotring has to be used instead.

      Parameters:
      services - The Services used by the Proof on which a Rule is applied right now.
      applicationPosInOccurrence - The PosInOccurrence in the previous Sequent which defines the JTerm that is rewritten.
      applicationTerm - The JTerm defined by the PosInOccurrence in the previous Sequent.
      rule - The Rule which is applied.
      goal - The optional Goal on which the JTerm to create will be used.
      hint - An optional hint passed from the active rule to describe the term which should be created.
      tacletTerm - The optional JTerm in the taclet which is responsible to instantiate the new JTerm for the new proof node or null in case of built in rules.
      newTerm - the template for the new JTerm to create
      childTerm - The JTerm which is a child or grandchild of the application JTerm that provides the TermLabel.
      label - The TermLabel to decide if it should be kept or dropped.
      Returns:
      true add TermLabel to new JTerm. false do not add TermLabel to new JTerm.
